<!-- echo block below verbatim; strip Read line numbers -->
Agent({team_name: "code-review-1337", name: "security-reviewer", subagent_type: "code-review-security", mode: "auto", description: "Code review specialist — security", prompt: "You are security-reviewer on the code review team for test-owner/test-repo PR #1337.\n\nASSIGNMENT_TASK_ID: 7\n\nYour first two actions are: (1) Read /tmp/pr-review-XXXXXX/spawn-context.md and (2) Read /tmp/pr-review-XXXXXX/rubric.md — both once, before anything else. Together they hold every per-PR value (HEAD_SHA, REPO_ROOT, REVIEW_TMPDIR, diff path, summary, changed files, roster, prior issues, CLAUDE.md content) plus the verbatim rubric. Do not Read any of the JSON artifacts (roster, prior-issues, claude-md-files, changed-files) separately — they are inside the bundle. The diff path is at the bundle's DIFF: header — do not Read or `cat` it separately.\n\nThe bundle embeds the contents of every changed file at HEAD (under `## Source at HEAD`) for files small enough to fit. Search that section before reaching for `git show` or `Read`. Only `git show` files that are NOT in the changed-files list (e.g. a callee file you need to verify a finding against), or files marked `_omitted: …_` because they exceeded the 32 KB embedding cap.\n\nWhen emitting `line` for a finding, Read the source file at HEAD to confirm the line number — never compute it from hunk-header arithmetic. Use `Bash: git show \u003cHEAD_SHA\u003e:\u003crepo-relative-path\u003e` for files at HEAD; never Read absolute paths from your cwd, because the cwd may be a worktree that is not checked out to HEAD. The bundle's `REPO_ROOT:` line is your handle for cross-file lookups: `Bash: git -C \u003cREPO_ROOT\u003e grep \u003csymbol\u003e \u003cHEAD_SHA\u003e -- '*.ts'` for HEAD-pinned symbol searches. **Never** run `find \u003crepo\u003e | xargs grep` — full-repo recursive scans can blow the team's 240 s safety budget on a large monorepo (transcript `65606fdb`, May 2026: typescript-reviewer paid two such scans recovering from absolute-path Reads).\n\nWrite `findings/security.json` via `Bash: cat \u003e /tmp/pr-review-XXXXXX/findings/security.json \u003c\u003c'EOF' … EOF` rather than the `Write` tool. A common third-party `PreToolUse:Write` hook substring-matches sensitive-API tokens in payload content; quoting source under review verbatim in your finding's `code` / `suggested_fix` fields will trip it, and the silent recovery path is to replace the offending lines with `...` placeholders — that is fidelity loss the user can't see. Bash heredoc is on a separate matcher and lets the source quote land intact.\n\nIf a Read returns `File content (… tokens) exceeds maximum allowed tokens (25000)` (rare on the bundle now that the rubric has been split out, but possible on a very large diff), retry with `offset: 0, limit: 200` and paginate."})

Monitor({command: "sleep 240; echo scan_complete_timer_fired", timeout_ms: 245000, persistent: false, description: "code-review scan-complete safety timer"})
